title: dForge Column Domains
description: domains.json — reusable, named semantic types. A domain bundles a base datatype, a control, sizing and a shared option list under one code; columns reference it via "domain": "module_cd.domain_cd" instead of restating the definition. Structural fields are materialized onto each column at install; options stay on the domain and resolve at metadata-load time, so a shared list is authored and translated exactly once.

propertyNames.description: Domain code. Must not contain '.', which is reserved for the qualified 'module_cd.domain_cd' form.

description.description: Human-readable label. Localizable via translations/<locale>.json under domains.<domain_cd>.label.

baseDatatypeCd.description: Base datatype the domain stands for (e.g. string, number, date, datetime, boolean). Required — it is what consuming columns' DDL is generated from.

dbDatatype.description: Physical Postgres type for consuming columns (e.g. varchar, int4, numeric). Omit to let it be inferred from fieldTypeCd.

fieldTypeCd.description: Control used to render/edit consuming columns (e.g. dropdown, radio, flags, text, number).

params.description: Field-type config shared by every consuming column — most importantly the 'options' list for dropdown/radio/flags. Never copied onto columns: it resolves from the domain at load time, which is what lets its labels be translated once for all consumers.

options.description: Option list. Each entry is either a bare string (value doubles as label) or an object. Per-locale label/icon/color overrides live in translations/<locale>.json under domains.<domain_cd>.options.<value>.

value.description: The stored code. Never translatable — translations match on it.
